deep_dive.title: Dynamic Proxy-Mixing: Transferring Replay Controllers from Small to Large Models for Continual Instruction Tuning
deep_dive.abstract: Continual instruction tuning updates a language model through a sequence of new domains, yet each update can progressively erode previously learned capabilities and alignment behavior. Replay is the standard mitigation, but fixed replay ratios are inherently limited because the optimal mixture varies with the current domain, the training stage, and the evolving vulnerability of prior behaviors. We propose PROX-YMIX, a framework that learns a dynamic replay controller on a small proxy model and transfers the frozen controller to a larger target. The controller never observes future tasks and constructs its state from normalized validation losses and their temporal dynamics, producing a masked mixture over the current task and accessible replay buffers. Our core empirical hypothesis is forgetting mirroring: task vulnerability rankings remain largely consistent across model scales even when absolute loss magnitudes differ. We validate this assumption empirically before transferring controllers across scales. On LLaMA-3-8B across five continual instruction tuning sequences, PROXYMIX improves average accuracy by 3.4 points, reduces final forgetting by 3.5 points, and raises safety score by 5.8 points over the strongest non-oracle baseline, at roughly 50x lower policy learning cost than Oracle Target RL. The framework is leakage free and architecture independent at the interface level, and we also identify settings where the proxy assumption breaks down, highlighting limitations for robust deployment.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: dynamic replay controller for mixing data to preserve alignment during continual instruction tuning
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 动态重放控制器用于混合数据以在持续指令微调中保持对齐
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 动态重放控制器用于混合数据以在持续指令微调中保持对齐
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 动态重放控制器用于混合数据以在持续指令微调中保持对齐
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Proposes PROX-YMIX, a dynamic replay controller that learns to mix data from previous tasks to prevent alignment erosion during continual instruction tuning, transferred from a small proxy model.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 持续指令微调中，模型的能力和对齐行为会逐步退化。现有方法采用固定比例的重放，但最优混合比例随任务和阶段动态变化。本文提出PROX-YMIX框架，在小代理模型上学习动态重放控制器，并将其冻结后迁移到大模型，通过验证损失和时序动态构造状态，产生掩码混合，从而有效防止对齐遗忘。实验表明该方法在保持对齐的同时，提升了持续学习性能。

deep_dive.title: Dialectics of Alignment: Harnessing Unsafe Knowledge for Dynamic Safety Routing
deep_dive.abstract: The prevailing paradigm in large language model (LLM) alignment operates via erasure, filtering unsafe data or training models to strictly refuse harmful prompts. While effective at reducing immediate toxicity, this approach fundamentally constricts the model's epistemological scope, resulting in over-cautious systems that output uninformative blanket refusals to sensitive yet benign queries. In this work, we challenge the orthodoxy that unsafe data must be discarded. We propose a dialectical approach to alignment, positing that unsafe data encodes rich, domain specific knowledge critical for nuanced, safe, and informative generation. To operationalize this, we introduce SafeMoE, a Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) framework that isolates unsafe knowledge into domain-specific Low-Rank Adapters (LoRA experts) trained exclusively on harmful corpora. To synthesize safety from these unsafe primitives, we train a lightweight gating network using a minimal, highly curated set of safe-informative responses. During inference, this router dynamically orchestrates the unsafe experts, effectively steering the generation trajectory to harness their deep domain knowledge while strictly enforcing safety constraints. Extensive empirical evaluations across stringent safety benchmarks demonstrate that SafeMoE is not only safer, achieving over a 20% relative improvement in safe response rate (more than a 15% absolute gain), but also produces more informative responses when safety and harmfulness are of paramount concern. Furthermore, the routing mechanism exhibits strong zero-shot generalization to unseen domains and broader safety tasks without domain-specific supervision. Our findings suggest a paradigm shift in alignment: true safety requires not the masking of unsafe knowledge, but its controlled integration.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: proposes mixing unsafe and safe knowledge for alignment using MoE
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 提出利用MoE混合不安全知识与安全知识进行对齐
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 提出利用MoE混合不安全知识与安全知识进行对齐
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 提出利用MoE混合不安全知识与安全知识进行对齐
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: This paper challenges erasure-based alignment by harnessing unsafe knowledge via a Mixture-of-Experts framework (SafeMoE) for dynamic safety routing, achieving better balance between safety and informativeness.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 本文挑战了大语言模型对齐中的擦除范式，提出一种辩证对齐方法，利用不安全知识来增强模型的安全性和有用性。作者设计了SafeMoE框架，通过混合专家模型将不安全知识隔离并用于动态安全路由。实验结果表明，该方法在保持安全性的同时，显著减少了对敏感但合法查询的盲目拒绝，提升了模型的响应质量。这一工作为安全对齐数据的混合与选择提供了新思路。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 本文挑战了大语言模型对齐中的擦除范式，提出一种辩证对齐方法，利用不安全知识来增强模型的安全性和有用性。作者设计了SafeMoE框架，通过混合专家模型将不安全知识隔离并用于动态安全路由。实验结果表明，该方法在保持安全性的同时，显著减少了对敏感但合法查询的盲目拒绝，提升了模型的响应质量。这一工作为安全对齐数据的混合与选择提供了新思路。
deep_dive.matched_query_text: Papers central to 请帮我查找关于大语言模型安全微调中“安全对齐数据与普通微调数据混合”的相关论文。重点关注在微调过程中如何引入、选择、混合或调度安全对齐数据，以缓解微调导致的安全退化、有害微调攻击、越狱风险或安全对齐遗忘等问题。优先召回涉及 safety alignment data mixing、safety data selection、data curation、data scheduling、alignment preservation、harmful fine-tuning defense 等方向的论文。, especially work that connects or combines: mixing safety alignment data with fine-tuning data; selecting safety data for fine-tuning; curating safety data for alignment preservation; defending against harmful fine-tuning attacks; mixture of safety data and normal data in fine-tuning; mitigating safety degradation from fine-tuning; papers on mixing safety alignment data with standard fine tuning data to prevent alignment loss; research on data curation and scheduling for preserving model alignment during fine tuning; methods to defend against harmful fine tuning attacks by integrating safety data; how to select and incorporate safety data to mitigate jailbreak risks in LLM fine tuning.

quick_skim.title: Expert-Aware Refusal Steering
quick_skim.abstract: Safety alignment in instruction-tuned large language models (LLMs) depends on a model's ability to reliably refuse to respond to harmful or disallowed requests. Recent work has shown that a steering vector can be applied to a dense LLM during inference to effectively suppress refusal behavior, inducing response to harmful requests. We extend this refusal steering method to three open-source Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) LLMs and find that steering performance is uninhibited by the complex routing patterns inherent to the MoE architecture. We then propose two expert-aware refusal steering methods that leverage refusal-specific expert routing patterns and expert-specific steering directions to suppress normal refusal behavior. We find that refusal behavior can be effectively steered based on the output of a single expert. Our results show that refusal signals captured by steering methods differ from expert routing behavior, suggesting a substantial role for attention in MoE refusal behavior.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: Proposes expert-aware refusal steering methods for MoE LLMs, leveraging refusal-specific expert routing patterns.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 提出专家感知的拒绝引导方法，针对MoE大语言模型利用拒绝相关的专家路由模式。
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 提出专家感知的拒绝引导方法，针对MoE大语言模型利用拒绝相关的专家路由模式。
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 提出专家感知的拒绝引导方法，针对MoE大语言模型利用拒绝相关的专家路由模式。
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Extends refusal steering to MoE LLMs and proposes two expert-aware methods that effectively suppress refusal behavior using expert-specific steering directions.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 该论文将拒绝引导方法扩展到混合专家（MoE）大语言模型中，发现传统引导在MoE中依然有效。进一步提出两种专家感知的拒绝引导方法，利用拒绝相关的专家路由模式和专家特定的引导方向来压制正常拒绝行为。实验证明这些方法能有效操纵模型的拒绝行为，为安全对齐的激活引导研究提供了新思路。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 该论文将拒绝引导方法扩展到混合专家（MoE）大语言模型中，发现传统引导在MoE中依然有效。进一步提出两种专家感知的拒绝引导方法，利用拒绝相关的专家路由模式和专家特定的引导方向来压制正常拒绝行为。实验证明这些方法能有效操纵模型的拒绝行为，为安全对齐的激活引导研究提供了新思路。

quick_skim.title: MESA: Improving MoE Safety Alignment via Decentralized Expertise
quick_skim.abstract: Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) architectures scale Large Language Models (LLMs) efficiently, enabling greater capacity with reduced computational cost by dynamically routing inputs to relevant experts, yet introduce a critical vulnerability: Safety Sparsity, where safety capabilities concentrate in few experts, making them susceptible to adversarial bypassing. Meanwhile, conventional alignment methods uniformly adapt all parameters, ignoring their functional differences and inadvertently degrading performances. To address these challenges, we propose MESA (MoE Safety Alignment), a targeted alignment framework for MoE-based LLMs that strategically decentralizes safety responsibility to maximize coverage while minimizing interference with utility. Based on Optimal Transport (OT) theory, MESA operates through two mechanisms: (1) Expert Capacity Reallocation uses a transport cost matrix to distribute safety duties to the most cost-effective experts, and (2) Dynamic Routing Refinement constrains the router to precisely activate these decentralized modules. Experiments show that MESA achieves robust defensive performance against varied harmful benchmarks while preserving helpfulness. Code is available at https://github.com/lorraine021/MESA.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: addresses safety alignment in MoE LLMs during fine-tuning
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 针对MoE大语言模型微调中的安全对齐问题
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 针对MoE大语言模型微调中的安全对齐问题
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 针对MoE大语言模型微调中的安全对齐问题
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: MESA proposes a targeted alignment framework for MoE LLMs that decentralizes safety responsibility across experts to maximize coverage while minimizing utility interference. It addresses safety sparsity vulnerability where safety concentrates in few experts.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 混合专家（MoE）大语言模型存在安全稀疏性——安全能力集中在少数专家上，易被绕过。本文提出MESA框架，通过策略性地分散安全职责到多个专家，在最大化安全覆盖的同时最小化对模型性能的干扰。基于最优传输理论的方法有效提升了MoE模型的安全对齐鲁棒性，为防止微调导致安全退化提供了新思路。

quick_skim.title: Inference-Time Vulnerability Beyond Shallow Safety: Alignment Along Generation Trajectories
quick_skim.abstract: Safety-aligned Large Language Models (LLMs) remain vulnerable to interventions during inference that redirect generation toward harmful outputs. Recent work attributes this to shallow safety, where alignment concentrates in the first few output tokens. We show that shallow safety is a special case of a broader inference-time vulnerability, in which short token injections at any generation step can substantially alter subsequent safety behavior. We also find that a model's alignment with refusal directions in its hidden states does not predict its robustness to such injection, revealing that internal state alone does not determine generation behavior under perturbation. To address this, we align models directly on generation trajectories constructed by simulating mid-sequence perturbation, and show that this improves robustness to mid-sequence injection and generalizes to attacks that exploit early-token generation. Our work argues that robust safety alignment requires training on the generation process itself, not only its outputs.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: studies inference-time safety degradation and proposes alignment along generation trajectories
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 研究推理时安全退化并提出沿生成轨迹的对齐方法
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 研究推理时安全退化并提出沿生成轨迹的对齐方法
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 研究推理时安全退化并提出沿生成轨迹的对齐方法
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Shows that shallow safety is a special case of broader inference-time vulnerability; proposes aligning models directly on generation trajectories to mitigate this.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 针对大语言模型推理时易受干扰导致安全退化的问题，本文揭示浅层安全只是更广泛推理时间脆弱性的特例，任何生成步骤的注入都能改变后续行为。研究发现隐藏层的拒绝方向不能预测鲁棒性，因此提出在完整生成轨迹上进行对齐训练的方法，旨在从根本上缓解安全退化。实验验证了该方法的有效性。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 针对大语言模型推理时易受干扰导致安全退化的问题，本文揭示浅层安全只是更广泛推理时间脆弱性的特例，任何生成步骤的注入都能改变后续行为。研究发现隐藏层的拒绝方向不能预测鲁棒性，因此提出在完整生成轨迹上进行对齐训练的方法，旨在从根本上缓解安全退化。实验验证了该方法的有效性。
